reviewTitle: It really works! (Updating my rating from 5 to 3)
reviewDescription: Ok I was weary about all these products and definitely felt overwhelmed. After the reviews and research I landed on this one. And listen, BUY IT! Skip the bleach, skip the drain cleaners at the store, skip the bug sprays. This truly works and I’m so thrilled that I’m getting my moneys worth! I am on day 4 of treating my drains and I see such a drastic change. Before this product I was overwhelmed with drain gnats, killing 6-7 just on every bathroom visit, getting harassed by gnats sitting on the toilet and brushing my teeth. This is day 4 at 2:50pm Eastern time lol I have only seen 1 gnat and it was struggling to move so it was easy to kill it. I’m not being dramatic and I swear I wasn’t paid for this review. This product works and I’m happy I found something that finally does. I spent hundreds on other products just the past two weeks and had I known, I would’ve bought this to start. BUY IT, STOCK IT, TELL EVERYONE! Please don’t change the formula because this is perfect! I will buy another gallon soon, but I still have about half of my gallon left after treating 8 drains for just 3 days so far. I love it thank you all so much, this has truly changed my household. I’m going to recommend this to everyone! Update: Ok so the gnats clearly haven’t went anywhere because I am back to killing multiple per bathroom visit. It’s so overwhelming, I’m going to have to try another product. I followed the directions and did the treatment everyday for 5 straight days, I didn’t do it yesterday and it’s like I never did it at all. It’s overwhelming to say the least.
Had to call a pest control company after trying the treatment, it partially worked, but still had drain flies
Did not work

reviewTitle: Does get rid of some fruitflies
reviewDescription: This is a 3.5 star. We poured some into two sinks and a shower in a bathroom that kept having fruit flies. It did work to reduce the amount of flies, but the time and amount required to fully eliminate is bit much given the cost of one bottle. Perhaps, a more concentrated solution that requires less time or better pricing on the current solution would be helpful.

The purpose of this review is to inform readers of my experience with this problem. Probably for the last 8-10 years, fruit flies and / or gnats have been the bane of my existence in the late summer and fall months. Last year it carried into Thanksgiving. Very very frustrating every year. After noticing the first sign of the emergence of these bugs I did everything possible to get rid of them but, from experience, knew I would have to suffer through it until they eventually died off. But this year that has all changed. I know everybody’s situation is unique, but I have found that, for me at least, my root cause all along has been my garbage can in my kitchen. My simplehuman trash can has been a literal petri dish for these flies. After disinfecting my garbage can thoroughly, I now keep a plastic store bag in my freezer and put all food related items in there and then deposit it at a trash can outside various establishments. The only thing I put in my garbage can is coffee grounds and non food items. I do have this drain fly product just in case but for now I think I’ve FINALLY solved my problem. I hope this has helped.

reviewTitle: WARNING: THIS PRODUCT MAY BE TOXIC TO PETS!!! WARNING
reviewDescription: The method did not work, and the video, along with the labeling instructions, is confusing. The video shows individuals using a ladle or a spoonful of the material, while the container instructions require pouring 8 ounces down the drain. I purchased two containers of the Fruit Fly Drain Treatment, but I find the product label misleading. It states “Non-toxic and Safe for Home," yet peppermint oil, one of the ingredients, is toxic to dogs. Please see the attached photos. WARNING! This product may be harmful to your pet. I am unsure why, at the time of this review, Rufus AI did not mention any potential dangers this product poses to pets.

So far I’ve had the most success with those old style, real sticky fly strips you hang up and vinegar with a few drops of dish detergent in a very small bowl, cover it with Saran Wrap and poke some holes in the wrap. But that doesn’t keep them from breeding. I’m looking into other items to stop them from breeding in my sink.

reviewTitle: Find the problem then they will disappear
reviewDescription: I purchased this and didn’t see any difference in the gnats in my house. I purchased the little bottles and caught many gnats. Then one day found the source of the problem…rotten g sweet potatoes and once those were thrown out my problem was solved.

Like one other reviewer stated, this is nothing but pouring money down the drain. Before using this, there was like 2, maybe 3 flies in my utility room, that I assumed came from the drain, but now, it's HORRIBLE. I killed 3 flies in my living room last night, 4 on my interior rear door's window this morning, and this afternoon, I just noticed there are like 5 or 6 buzzing around one of my basement windows. This is not killong anything; all this is making the flies do, is not want to be in the drain. It's truly disgusting. Even if one of my daughters stood at the door with it wide open, we would never have this many flies in the house. Terrible product. I feel misled and ROBBED of money!

reviewTitle: Skip, buy from the next listing!!!
reviewDescription: It is a certainty this review will never be seen, I am convinced. One of the reasons I never ever leave reviews! However, in this case it will allow me to vent. Something so simple yet so frustrating. I love the design, because I will use them prophylactically, not for treatment and I can use one tiny flag per plant. In addition, I have a terrarium that does not require the prescribed configuration. To make the LSS, you get 30 flags 8 stakes. What?! Do the math! What?! A smart company would pack extras in case of breakage, losses… or good marketing! But, fine! I bought from a company that didn’t agree with my marketing philosophy. I got on Amazon searching to purchase more stakes…guess what? Nah, nah, nah, no stakes! This is my beef: now I have to buy the entire “Sha Bang!” again! This co. Knows it! Oh, I read the label more closely... I’m also missing some zip ties of some sort (included a picture of box) this tiny thing, has left me with the need for Xanax. Skip, buy from the next listing!

Maybe if were clips it would definitely be much better idea. For me I’m not getting it again. I wouldn’t recommend getting this even though I like how you can connect them and make them talk, but with the way the stripes come undone no thanks.

reviewDescription: Not worth it. Just get the normal kind. These are fragile and the bottom sticky trap is so close to the tip it just goes straight into the dirt. I am having buyers remorse by buying something other than what I know works. Don't do it!

This product was re-ordered according to my purchase history, but the 2nd order appeared to reflect a different brand name/supplier even though packaging details indicate they’re identical. In the photo included, the brand on the left (from 1st order) was much easier to assemble even with the adhesive coating on the yellow strips (folded to mimic flower petals). The package on the right is ridiculously sticky and nearly impossible to control while trying to fold and insert into the slots on the green stakes. In fact, without extreme caution you’re likely to pull skin off fingers while trying to fold over to stick ends together. Even the release paper was difficult to peel off each strip. The results aren’t worth the effort or frustration as they pop apart anyway, and seem to adhere to skin much more effectively than their own edges.
Did get rid of aphids

reviewDescription: If you have time to play with double stick tape on flimsy plastic then this product may be for you. Unfortunately I was not happy with this product. reviewDescription: Well it's really cute, and easy to use, however I have found two fruit flies in my coffee again and still none have been attracted to the little sticky gadget.
These were good in theory, but in use, they completely failed. I gave them two stars for trying. I do not recommend. :(

reviewTitle: Good for indoor use, not outdoors
reviewDescription: I was hoping to be able to kill the flies that fly around in circles on my patio, but it is too difficult to swat them. So, if you are buying this for indoors, I think it works well, but not for outdoor use.

reviewTitle: Just ok, not as strong of a zap as others
reviewDescription: I got these because the other ones I had from a different brand got recalled. These are definitely not as strong. I thought I’d like how they fold but it’s actually really annoying because it doesn’t lock into place. I’ll go to swing for a fly and it will fold up on me. While it does do what it advertises, there are some design flaws that need to be fixed in order for it to function better.

There were two in the set, so I gave one to my granddaughter. The first time I used mine it started burning, and sparked. Every time I tried to use it, it smelled like electrical burning. I won’t use it now.

reviewTitle: Need to Recharge It Often. It Does Work
reviewDescription: It works but you may get 3 hours max of runtime before you have to recharge it, even on standby (no zapping). It takes about 3 hours to charge it. It works for fruit flies. The zapping sound is loud enough that it makes us jump. I would've gone with another brand in hindsight. If it zaps a few bugs it does smell (that's expected from any of these). I'd prefer one that has a stand.
Product works well, however, one of the swatters does not fully charge.

reviewTitle: Immensely satisfying when it works, which is sadly just half the time
reviewDescription: Only kills flies like half the time on the first zap. It’s immensely satisfying, when it works though. As a result, 2.5 stars trending towards 3 with the satisfaction. If it was powerful enough to kill all, or most, the first time, it would be 5 stars.

They do work and certainly when they’re in the standing position with the light on, attract insects and kill them. However, the battery needs to be charged daily and if you’re using the lighted feature, it does not last very long. Also, when in the normal racket position, the swivel does not stay secure and allows the head portion to move. There should be a lock to make them more solid.

I think the safety screen keeps the bigger flies from getting through to the live wires. I laid the racket across a bowl of wet cat food and the flies walked across the safety grid …and not one was zapped. It works great for the tiny flies that circle around my porch and never seem to land. I like the rechargeable battery a lot.
Works okay, mainly stuns the flies rather than kills them. Also loses its charge relatively fast so I am having to charge it after every use
The swiveling pad rotates when when your swatting flys ,nice concept but poorly executed Unit stops working after few days

reviewTitle: Good idea, if they only caught gnats
reviewDescription: I bought these because I thought there may be more surface area and I liked the pegs they were on. At first I thought “great, there’s nothing on here, so I must’ve solved my net problem!” But, they just are not catching anything. Side-by-side with the regular yellow gnat traps I use, the others are full of gnats and these only have one or two. Will be returning the remainder.

reviewTitle: Don’t do it!! Not outdoors anyway!!!
reviewDescription: I used these as instructed to try to get rid of a terrible infestation of thrips. It certainly did capture thrips but after 24 hours of placing them, I went outside and to my horror, dozens of lizards were stuck to these awful traps. Then when trying to figure out how to save the few living ones, I read how awful and inhumane they are. In the end, dozens of lizards died. I was able to save 3 or 4 only. And I feel like the worst human on earth. How awful it was to see. Please do yourself a favor and use these inside only. You never know what might be in your garden and they’re powerfully sticky and strong enough to trap large anole lizards and who knows what else. It was a truly horrifying experience.

Very cheap, not well thought out design. The sticks are very short to where the sticky paper has to basically touch the dirt. In the product pictures, the sticky papers are shown folded over onto itself, then placed onto the slit, on the stake. The ends of the sticky paper do not have the glue on them so they do not stay folded over! They pop out and look so tacky and messy. Very unstable and they fall into the dirt. I'm using these outside to try to catch white flies in my organic garden. I don't doubt that the sticky paper can catch some bugs, but it would be nice if they could come with better holders! These were frustrating to use. Not sure how long they are going to last honestly.

reviewTitle: Messy and does not stay folded!!
reviewDescription: Thought I would try these sticky traps out instead of the type that I usually buy whichbqrebthe type you stick directly into the soil. Big mistake! I regretted it!! The sticky strips do not remain folded, keep popping open and falling off of the stick. Not to mention that you have to deal with all the sticky residue all over your hands as you struggle with it. I DO NOT recommend this product. After several attempts with just 2 sticks and 4 strips I ended up just opening the strips and placing them on top of the soil just to not waste my money. I absolutely will not get these again.

reviewTitle: Stupidest thing I ever bought on Amazon
reviewDescription: I was excited to get these! They were much harder to assemble than I imagined. The directions were awful. The first one I assembled, I didn't realize that there was protective paper on BOTH SIDES that needed to be removed! Subsequent ones stuck to my fingers and kept popping off the little green sticks! The directions say "hang at plant level with supplied twist tie from branch or stake" There were no twist ties supplied in the box. I used a hole punch (before removing protective paper), and then string, to hang these. (While squeezing a hole punch, I managed to punch a bloody hole in the palm of my hand -- my fault, but I shouldn't have had to use a hole punch in the first place.) Frustrating. Won't buy again.

reviewTitle: These look like they will work but DON’T
reviewDescription: These do not do the job, unlike the other products for this purpose that I previously used by Safer Home. I purchased them because I needed more yellow strips and this company offered the 90 pack. I have been battling fungal gnats on my half dozen indoor plants. After installing these new catchers across many of my plants and waiting more than 24 hours, there are very few gnats on them but many more captured on the few remaining Safer Home versions. I know this sounds like an ad for the other company, but it is 100% true that now I will throw the remainder of these away and have to repurchase from the first company to treat my problem. Don’t make the mistake I made. Don’t buy this ineffective product!

I put several of these traps in 5 of my potted plants. These traps remained clean for days despite visible fungus gnats and house flies buzzing everywhere. Finally, I just threw them away and replaced them with another brand. I am unimpressed and will not purchase these again.
These do not stick to the little posts. The sticky pads open up and flop over everywhere. They stick to leaves and the pots when they flop over. Useless

Very upset and disappointed with this product. The sticky tape is barely sticky at all, and gives the impression that the tiny bit of stickiness it does have will dry up in a day. I received and set it up today, then watched as gnats and fruit flies (living in the soil of my houseplants) walked all across the tape of all the traps with no problem, before flying off. They are not sticking whatsoever. This is totally useless. I will take a video of this and upload it tomorrow. I bought another brand before this one that was twice the price and worked perfectly-- the tape of that brand was very, very sticky (and has caught tons of bugs). I guess you get what you pay for.
Waist of money. Love the idea but it's not worth to spend money. The yellow strips won't stay and keep falling off
It does not work well

reviewTitle: These stickys need a better system to work like they should.
reviewDescription: The Yellow Sticky Traps for gnats and other small flying insects would catch the little buggers better if after removing the paper backings from both sides, the sticky tape would stick to itself when trying to bend them while attaching to the little sticks as they are shown in the illustrations. I had to cut them in half lengthways so that they would hold together better. But even then, after a few seconds, most would un-attach themselves where they were supposed to stick. Removing the paper from each side was a breeze, but the whole system of bending the stickys, and then holding the sticky to itself was a nightmare. And even if you could get them to stick together for any length of time, the little attachment on the stick, does not hold very well. They keep falling off. If the stickys were a bit thinner, I think that they would do much better, but the way they are, they are too thick to stay stuck to themselves.

I also reported the problem to management who said they would report it to their pest management people. The flies are mostly gone now.

First, the product is not that easy to use. The instructions say to shake well before opening, but it creates a layer of foamy bubbles that take about 15 minutes to dissolve. While the bubbles are present, the fruit flies don't go anywhere close to it. Then the whole product is a jar of some vinegar liquid that is just supposed to be left open on the counter 24 hours a day until all the flies are trapped. It's really easy to tip/spill every where and at 5.4 fl oz, it makes a mess. Second, the scent is awful. reviewTitle: I was extremely dissappointed in this product
reviewDescription: I read the directions twice and it is very simple. Shake real good before opening then unscrew the lid off and peel off the protective foil. Then just leave it sitting out on a counter or whatever, near where the fruit flies or gnats seem to be the worse. I would watch the fruit flies crawl all over this product kinda checking it out but it seemed they would always lose interest and go away instead of going for the liquid to get trapped. Now to be fair, I may have caught close to 50 after a couple weeks but when I saw it wasn't working to well, I broke out some fly ribbon paper I had leftover in an old box and the fly ribbon was absolutely covered with them so I won't be wasting any more money on traps like this when I can get 10pc of fly ribbon paper for real cheap. I even have a trick I use. When you hang the flypaper from the ceiling it doesn't really hang down real low and only traps the ones flying up high so I used a regular stick pin and hung a second strip from the one already hanging from the ceiling by sticking the pin into the side of the base, not the bottom, the bottom is too thin to hold it but if you stick it in the side, near the bottom it holds great and now you have two connected that span from the ceiling to about waist level so keep that in mind when doubling them up so as not to place them where you will be bumping into them as they are quite sticky. I really don't like to get less than positive reviews but don't waste your money on this. reviewTitle: Killt Insekten zuverlässig, aber Akku beim UV-Licht Betrieb sehr schnell alle.
reviewDescription: Wer Spaß am bruzeln von lästigen Insekten hat, für den ist dieser elektrische Helfer vielleicht genau das Richtige. Insekten wie Fliegen, Mücken und Spinnen werden schnell und zuverlässig getötet. Das Geräusch das dabei entsteht ist für manche sehr befriedigend, könnte aber für schreckhafte Personen zu laut sein. Wenn der Körper eines insektes auf das stromführende Gitter im Inneren trifft, explodiert er sofort. Dies verursacht - für mein Empfinden - ein ziemlich lautes Knallgeräusch. Positiv: Durch die integrierte UV-Lampe werden Insekten magisch angezogen und schnell erledigt. Ganz ohne Chemie. Durch den Scharniere Mechanismus, lässt sich das Gerät auf einer ebenen Fläche aufstellen, so dass man es nicht zwingend in der Hand halten muss um es zu nutzen. Negativ: leider ist der Akku - wider Erwarten - ziemlich schwach, und entlädt sich bei gleichzeitigem Betrieb der UV Lampe innerhalb von 1 bis 2 Stunden komplett. Das ist zu wenig und zwingt fast zum stationären Einsatz mit einem entsprechenden Netzteil. (Nicht mit im Lieferumfang) Aus diesem Grund kann ich leider nur drei Sterne vergeben. Wäre die Akkulaufzeit besser würde ich viereinhalb Sterne vergeben. Wenn du meine Rezension als nützlich empfandest, freue ich mich sehr über deine Bewertung.

I have used electric fly swatters in the past and found them to be very good. However, they have always needed batteries. This updated Yissvic 4000v Electric Fly Swatter has an internal rechargeable battery using a USB-A to USB-C cable which is supplied. It arrives in a brown box and initially looks like there may be some self assembly required. But this is not the case as the handle is hinged and just folds out. A full charge takes about 3-4 hours. It has a 1800mAh battery so a full charge should last a while. The instruction booklet recommends recharging every 3 months. On the side of the handle is an on/off switch which pushes further along for auto zapping mode. Another button can be pressed for manual zapping. The 'electrified' elements have a wire grid over them to prevent your fingers from touching them. The grid is big enough to allow flys and mosquitoes through. There are LED indicators on the handle for remaining battery life which is useful. The racket lights up purple when switched on. There is also a wrist strap attached so you don't accidentally throw it across the room. No storage bag included. I have two problems with this fly swatter. The first is that mine was damaged. The packaging was intact so this speaks to the manufacturers quality control. My second issue is with the folding handle which doesn't lock. This means that when you swipe at a bug the head can fold back on the handle. I don't think it is badly priced and I will be using it over the summer.

reviewTitle: RAQUETA ELÉCTRICA CON MARGEN DE MEJORA
reviewDescription: La YISSVIC Raqueta Mosquitos Eléctrica tiene varias características que, en principio, la hacen parecer una gran opción para quienes buscan combatir insectos en casa o al aire libre. Su diseño plegable, combinado con la función de lámpara UV, la hace versátil y fácil de usar. Sin embargo, después de algunas semanas de uso, he notado ciertos aspectos que podrían mejorarse. En cuanto a su rendimiento, aunque la raqueta promete eliminar insectos voladores con sus 4000V de potencia, la realidad es que no siempre es tan eficaz como esperaba. Si bien funciona bien con moscas y otros insectos más grandes, los mosquitos a veces logran escapar, incluso cuando están claramente cerca de la rejilla electrificada. La luz UV de 365 nm, que supuestamente atrae a los mosquitos de manera eficiente, no siempre cumple con su cometido, especialmente en espacios donde hay otras fuentes de luz. El hecho de que sea recargable por USB-C es un punto positivo, ya que evita el uso de baterías desechables. Sin embargo, la duración de la batería puede resultar decepcionante. A pesar de tener una batería de 1800 mAh, no dura tanto como se esperaría si se usa la función de lámpara con frecuencia, lo que me ha obligado a cargarla más de lo que me gustaría. Aunque las luces indicadoras de carga son útiles, el hecho de tener que cargarla tan seguido reduce su conveniencia. Otro aspecto que podría mejorar es el modo automático. Aunque la idea de dejarla como lámpara antimosquitos suena bien en teoría, no he visto grandes resultados en la práctica. Los mosquitos no siempre se ven atraídos por la luz, y en algunas noches he terminado teniendo que usar la raqueta manualmente para deshacerme de los insectos. Además, aunque el diseño plegable es práctico para ahorrar espacio, siento que la estabilidad de la lámpara en modo automático podría ser mejor, ya que a veces parece algo endeble. En cuanto a la seguridad, el diseño de tres capas de redes protectoras es una buena adición, especialmente para quienes tienen niños en casa, ya que reduce el riesgo de descargas accidentales.

We get a lot of bugs in the summer, and it’s good to have several of these electric fly swatters since sometimes when you want to use one, it is either broken or is out of power. I thought this one was cool, since it’s foldable, but it gives more grief than I bargained for. The design is so inconvenient, if you want to unfold it, you may accidentally end up zapping yourself instead of the fly you wanted to attack. Of course, the electric zap is not enough to do anything to a human, but it’s still unpleasant. I also think that this zapper is more flashy and not much in the way of function. There is a loop you can hang it from a hook, and turn on the purple light, and let the insects get attracted to it and be zapped to death, but it’s not practical, and the battery life on this is not good enough to be used like this. You have to charge this every few days, and it takes about 2-3 hours to fully charge. The sound of bugs getting zapped is loud! But depending on your insect, you may have poor success. This is better for the small critters like gnats and mosquitos, they definitely die on shock, but flies and larger insects will maybe be stunned or have no effect at all. You may have to keep zapping to try killing it and at this point, it’s better to just stomp on the insect or whack it with a book to save time. Even though it’s called a fly swatter, it doesn’t have the air resistance needed to effectively squash an insect (since its intended function is to zap). It is better used like a trap or something you push onto the insect so it willingly goes into the electric field itself, rather than you trying to bring the field to it. I don’t really recommend this item, unfortunately.

However, the UV light is a bit dim, so it didn’t attract insects as effectively as I hoped. The power slider feels too loose, making it easy to accidentally turn on. It also tips over easily if you try to stand it upright, but you can bend and rotate the handle to sit on its side, which helps. The rotatable handle is great for compact travel; it fits nicely in a backpack and is convenient to bring along. Overall, it has its flaws, but it gets the job done!
